skill.name: system-prompt
skill.description: Guides edits to DBX Studio AI system prompts, including prompt locations, response rules, tool order, schema context, and SQL safety expectations.
skill.summary: Keep DBX Studio AI chat prompts consistent across streaming and tool-based providers.
skill.author: Dbxstudio

security_audit.summary: Static command, crypto, sensitive-file, and path traversal alerts were reviewed against SKILL.md and are mostly Markdown or text-reference false positives. The real risk is semantic: this community skill guides changes to application system prompts and references repository files, so it should be published with a prompt-safety warning.

medium_findings.title: System Prompt Modification Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill is explicitly intended to edit DBX Studio AI system prompts and preserve prompt behavior across two application paths. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but prompt changes can affect model behavior, tool use, and query safety.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly documents where and how to edit production and provider prompts. The behavior is expected for the skill, but the blast radius justifies a medium warning.

medium_findings.title: Embedded System Prompt Text
medium_findings.description: The file includes reference prompt text beginning with an assistant role statement and response rules. I found no evidence that it tries to override the audit, but consumers should treat this as quoted prompt material rather than higher-priority instructions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The role text is present inside a documented prompt structure, not as an instruction to the reviewer. It is still relevant because embedded prompt text can influence downstream agents if context boundaries are weak.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick patterns correspond to inline code spans, fenced code blocks, and Markdown examples. No evidence found of executable scripts, shell commands, or command construction in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious characters are visible as Markdown formatting and documentation snippets. There is no surrounding code capable of executing them.

low_findings.title: Repository-Relative Links Trigger Path Traversal Alerts
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings are Markdown links to DBX Studio source files outside the skill folder. They are not file reads or writes, but they identify files that a user may edit.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The traversal sequences appear only inside Markdown link targets. They still support the filesystem risk factor because the skill directs edits to repository files.

low_findings.title: Sensitive File And Weak Crypto Alerts Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM and weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not correspond to sensitive file access or cryptographic operations in the reviewed file. No evidence found of credential access, hashing, encryption, or secret handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose, headings, or variable names. I found no code path that accesses SAM data or invokes weak cryptographic primitives.

content.value_statement: DBX Studio prompt changes can drift across streaming and tool-based chat paths. This skill maps prompt locations and rules for consistent edits.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the streaming prompt in ai-stream.ts.
Identifies the oRPC provider prompt in providersWithTools.ts.
Documents the expected tool order for schema, query, chart, and table tasks.
Explains response rules for concise SQL assistant answers.
Reminds editors to keep schema context and scoped schema lines.
Describes query safety expectations for SELECT and WITH workflows.
limitations: It does not include automated tests or validation scripts.
It does not edit files by itself.
It is specific to DBX Studio prompt locations and naming.
It does not verify database permissions or runtime tool behavior.

use_cases.title: Align Chat Prompt Behavior
use_cases.description: Update both DBX Studio chat prompt paths so users receive consistent SQL assistant behavior.

use_cases.title: Review Tool Use Rules
use_cases.description: Check that schema lookup, query execution, and chart generation instructions stay in the intended order.

use_cases.title: Improve Data Assistant Responses
use_cases.description: Refine response style rules so database answers remain direct, concise, and grounded in tool results.

prompt_templates.title: Find Prompt Locations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the system-prompt skill to show where DBX Studio stores its AI chat system prompts.

prompt_templates.title: Update Response Tone
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the system-prompt skill to make DBX Studio AI answers more concise while preserving tool use rules.

prompt_templates.title: Synchronize Provider Prompts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the system-prompt skill to compare the streaming and oRPC prompt guidance and propose matching edits.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Query Safety Instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the system-prompt skill to review DBX Studio query safety rules and identify prompt changes that preserve SELECT-only behavior.

output_examples.input: Make DBX Studio's AI assistant less verbose.
output_examples.output: A concise edit plan that updates response style rules while preserving schema lookup, tool order, and SQL safety guidance.

output_examples.input: Where should I update the provider prompt?
output_examples.output: A short explanation naming the oRPC provider prompt location and why it should stay aligned with the streaming prompt.

output_examples.input: Review the prompt for chart behavior.
output_examples.output: A focused checklist covering chart selection guidance, query prerequisites, and response formatting expectations.

best_practices: Update both prompt paths when changing shared assistant behavior.
Keep schema context and scoped schema text intact.
Test prompt changes with real database questions before release.
anti_patterns: Do not remove tool-use rules that prevent schema guessing.
Do not add broad instructions that allow unsafe write queries.
Do not edit only one prompt path when behavior should match.

faq.question: What does this skill help edit?
faq.answer: It helps edit DBX Studio AI chat system prompts and their response rules.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file is documentation and prompt guidance only.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: It guides system prompt changes, which can affect model behavior and tool use.

faq.question: Can it update prompts automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives locations and rules, but file edits require a user or agent.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it specific to DBX Studio?
faq.answer: Yes. The paths and prompt rules reference DBX Studio's project structure.
